name: Kingdom Community Development Corporation
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit housing counselor in Fayetteville, NC offering pre-purchase counseling, foreclosure prevention, and affordable housing development at no cost.
description_long:
Kingdom Community Development Corporation is a HUD-approved non-profit housing counseling agency based in Fayetteville, North Carolina, with a mission centered on building affordable housing and strengthening low to moderate-income communities. The organization operates as a community development corporation dedicated to expanding homeownership access and economic opportunity in Fayetteville and surrounding counties.

The organization offers a range of housing-related services including pre-purchase counseling for first-time homebuyers, foreclosure prevention assistance, budget counseling, and property management. They host First-Time Homebuyer Workshops in partnership with the City of Fayetteville, and advertise down payment assistance and IDA (Individual Development Account) programs. Beyond counseling, Kingdom CDC actively develops affordable housing through projects like Fairley Estates, Mack Street, and Elizabeth Street properties, demonstrating hands-on community revitalization work.

Kingdom CDC's distinction lies in its dual focus: combining HUD-certified housing counseling with direct affordable housing development and property ownership. This positions them as both an advisor and builder, not merely a counselor. They also partner with the NC Community Development Initiative and Fayetteville Technical Community College to amplify their reach and resources. The organization emphasizes youth education and business development training alongside housing services.

A significant limitation is that detailed service pages (pre-purchase counseling and foreclosure prevention) return 404 errors on their website, making it difficult to assess specific program depth, eligibility requirements, or outcomes. While the organization clearly operates and hosts events, potential clients cannot access complete service descriptions online. The geographic focus (Fayetteville and surrounding NC counties) also limits accessibility for consumers outside this region.
pros: HUD-approved housing counselor status ensures compliance with federal standards and consumer protections
Offers down payment assistance programs and IDA accounts to help first-time buyers overcome financial barriers
Free or low-cost budget counseling and pre-purchase education workshops reduce cost barriers to homeownership
Combines counseling with direct affordable housing development, providing both guidance and tangible community properties
Partners with City of Fayetteville and Fayetteville Technical Community College for expanded resources and credibility
Addresses foreclosure prevention specifically, serving homeowners in distress beyond just first-time buyers
Demonstrates 20+ years of active development with multiple completed housing projects
cons: Critical service pages (pre-purchase counseling, foreclosure prevention details) are non-functional 404 errors, blocking access to key program information
Geographic limitation to Fayetteville and surrounding North Carolina counties only; not available nationally or online
Website lacks specific program details such as eligibility requirements, costs, timelines, or success metrics
No clear contact information, phone number, or appointment scheduling visible on main pages
No evidence of digital or remote counseling options for consumers unable to travel to Fayetteville
best_for: First-time homebuyers in the Fayetteville, NC area seeking free HUD-approved counseling and down payment assistance
Low to moderate-income homeowners facing foreclosure who need prevention counseling and options
Individuals seeking budget counseling and financial education paired with affordable housing pathways in central North Carolina
diagnosis: Kingdom CDC is best for first-time homebuyers and homeowners in financial distress located in the Fayetteville, NC area who need free, HUD-approved housing counseling combined with down payment assistance. The main caveat is that their website lacks functional service details and appears to serve only a geographic region of North Carolina, limiting accessibility for consumers outside this area or those seeking detailed online information before contacting the organization.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 129 N Main St, Spring Lake, NC 28390
